title: Maradona vs Pele
shortAnswer: Pele leads in goals and World Cups (3 vs 1), while Maradona dominated tournaments single-handedly. Both are legendary GOAT contenders with different strengths.

keyDifferences.label: Individual Impact
keyDifferences.entityAValue: Part of strong teams in multiple World Cups
keyDifferences.entityBValue: Carried Argentina almost single-handedly in 1986 and 1990

keyDifferences.label: World Cup Role
keyDifferences.entityAValue: Not the most important player in 1958 and 1962 wins
keyDifferences.entityBValue: Argentina's only standout player in World Cup campaigns

prosA: Superior goal scoring record with 757 career goals
Three World Cup victories spanning multiple eras
Exceptional goals per game ratio of 0.84
Scored in multiple World Cup finals
consA: Was not the most important player in 1958 and 1962 World Cup wins
Benefited from stronger team support throughout career
prosB: Single-handedly carried Argentina to World Cup glory in 1986
Demonstrated exceptional individual brilliance and leadership
Reached two consecutive World Cup finals as the key player
Showed ability to elevate team performance through individual skill
consB: Significantly lower goal scoring record compared to Pele
Only won one World Cup despite individual brilliance
Much lower goals per game ratio at 0.37
verdict: While both players are legitimate GOAT candidates, the statistics clearly favor Pele in terms of goals, trophies, and World Cup success. However, Maradona's ability to single-handedly dominate tournaments and carry his team makes him unique. Pele's consistency and team success edge out Maradona's individual brilliance in the overall comparison.
